vuln_q_0000_0000.title: python.django.security.audit.unvalidated-password.unvalidated-password
vuln_q_0000_0000.description: The password on 'password_manager' is being set without validating the password. Call django.contrib.auth.password_validation.validate_password() with validation functions before setting the password. See https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.0/topics/auth/passwords/ for more information.

vuln_q_0005_0004.title: python.lang.security.audit.dynamic-urllib-use-detected.dynamic-urllib-use-detected
vuln_q_0005_0004.description: Detected a dynamic value being used with urllib. urllib supports 'file://' schemes, so a dynamic value controlled by a malicious actor may allow them to read arbitrary files. Audit uses of urllib calls to ensure user data cannot control the URLs, or consider using the 'requests' library instead.

vuln_q_0009_0004.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml.use-defused-xml
vuln_q_0009_0004.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.

vuln_q_0014_0004.title: python.flask.security.audit.directly-returned-format-string.directly-returned-format-string
vuln_q_0014_0004.description: Detected Flask route directly returning a formatted string. This is subject to cross-site scripting if user input can reach the string. Consider using the template engine instead and rendering pages with 'render_template()'.

vuln_q_0017_0000.title: python.flask.security.audit.directly-returned-format-string.directly-returned-format-string
vuln_q_0017_0000.description: Detected Flask route directly returning a formatted string. This is subject to cross-site scripting if user input can reach the string. Consider using the template engine instead and rendering pages with 'render_template()'.

vuln_q_0021_0001.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml.use-defused-xml
vuln_q_0021_0001.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.

vuln_q_0021_0001.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml-parse.use-defused-xml-parse
vuln_q_0021_0001.description: The native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks.  These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.

vuln_q_0021_0004.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.

vuln_q_0021_0004.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml-parse.use-defused-xml-parse

These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.

vuln_q_0040_0000.title: python.flask.security.audit.app-run-param-config.avoid_app_run_with_bad_host
vuln_q_0040_0000.description: Running flask app with host 0.0.0.0 could expose the server publicly.

vuln_q_0045_0001.title: python.django.security.audit.unvalidated-password.unvalidated-password
vuln_q_0045_0001.description: The password on 'user' is being set without validating the password. Call django.contrib.auth.password_validation.validate_password() with validation functions before setting the password. See https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.0/topics/auth/passwords/ for more information.

vuln_q_0069_0000.title: python.cryptography.security.mode-without-authentication.crypto-mode-without-authentication
vuln_q_0069_0000.description: An encryption mode of operation is being used without proper message authentication. This can potentially result in the encrypted content to be decrypted by an attacker. Consider instead use an AEAD mode of operation like GCM.

vuln_q_0071_0001.title: python.django.security.audit.unvalidated-password.unvalidated-password
vuln_q_0071_0001.description: The password on 'new_user' is being set without validating the password. Call django.contrib.auth.password_validation.validate_password() with validation functions before setting the password. See https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.0/topics/auth/passwords/ for more information.

vuln_q_0071_0003.title: python.flask.security.audit.render-template-string.render-template-string
vuln_q_0071_0003.description: Found a template created with string formatting. This is susceptible to server-side template injection and cross-site scripting attacks.

vuln_q_0071_0003.title: python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled.debug-enabled
vuln_q_0071_0003.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

vuln_q_0077_0003.title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-pickle
vuln_q_0077_0003.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.

vuln_q_0077_0003.title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-pickle
vuln_q_0077_0003.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.

vuln_q_0085_0001.title: python.lang.security.audit.eval-detected.eval-detected
vuln_q_0085_0001.description: Detected the use of eval(). eval() can be dangerous if used to evaluate dynamic content. If this content can be input from outside the program, this may be a code injection vulnerability. Ensure evaluated content is not definable by external sources.

vuln_q_0086_0003.title: python.flask.security.audit.app-run-param-config.avoid_app_run_with_bad_host
vuln_q_0086_0003.description: Running flask app with host 0.0.0.0 could expose the server publicly.

vuln_q_0096_0002.title: python.lang.security.insecure-hash-algorithms-md5.insecure-hash-algorithm-md5
vuln_q_0096_0002.description: Detected MD5 hash algorithm which is considered insecure. MD5 is not collision resistant and is therefore not suitable as a cryptographic signature. Use SHA256 or SHA3 instead.
